title: Stock Market Highlights, July 6: Sensex jumps 521 points, Nifty closes above 24,430 - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Bull and Bear Stock Market Prices Concept. 3d Render | Photo Credit: asbe

Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Indian benchmark indices closed higher on Monday, buoyed by a sharp rally in HDFC Bank after its strong June-quarter business update. Lower crude oil prices and easing tensions in the Middle East also boosted investor sentiment.

The Sensex climbed 521.16 points (0.67%) to finish at 78,285.07, while the Nifty advanced 159.50 points (0.66%) to 24,430.35. Additional optimism stemmed from a narrowing monsoon rainfall deficit and indications that foreign investors are reconsidering their India exposure after months of outflows.

Banking stocks led the gains, with HDFC Bank jumping 3.6%, joined by advances in IndusInd Bank, Bandhan Bank, and Karur Vysya Bank. In contrast, Kotak Mahindra Bank declined after reporting weaker business growth, and PSU banks extended their losses.

Fourteen out of sixteen sectoral indices closed in positive territory, though IT stocks slipped ahead of the earnings season.

The rupee depreciated 0.2% against the U.S. dollar, and overall market breadth remained negative despite the benchmark gains.

* Sensex: +521.16 points (0.67%) to 78,285.07

* Nifty: +159.50 points (0.66%) to 24,430.35

Indian corporates are leveraging arbitrage opportunities, exacerbating the rupee's decline amid rising dollar demand.

Sensex settled 521.16 points or 0.67% higher at 78,285.07, and Nifty 50 ended 159.50 points or 0.66% positive at 24,430.35.

Essar Energy Transition has renewed US$300m crude facility between Essar Energy Transition Fuels and Petraco Oil Company SA demonstrating market confidence in the company’s performance, market position and strategic importance.

Essar Energy Transition Fuels, owner and operator of the Stanlow Refinery UK, is delivering strong commercial performance, following a landmark investment year with Essar Energy Transition Fuels also achieving its highest ever domestic sales and production since acquisition. It is investing in low-carbon energy solutions and the decarbonisation of its industrial assets.

The company has renewed a three-year, US$300m strategic crude and product facility. The transaction represents an important next step as Essar Energy Transition Fuel’s strengthens strategic relationships with leading industry players, like Petraco Oil Company SA. Petraco has been a valuable business partner for Essar over the years.

Fitch Ratings has upgraded India-based JSW Steel Limited’s (JSWS) Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) and the rating on JSWS’s outstanding bonds to ‘BB+’, from ‘BB’. All ratings have been removed from Rating Watch Positive (RWP). The Outlook is Positive.

Despite the broader market remaining strong, the IT sector continues to lag behind. At this stage, we suggest investors to avoid fresh exposure to most IT stocks and use any meaningful rally as an opportunity to exit rather than chase prices. In our view, the impact of Artificial Intelligence is not a temporary challenge but a structural shift for the industry. AI is likely to automate a large part of traditional IT services over the next few years, which may permanently reduce the valuation premium many IT companies enjoyed for decades. While a handful of companies may successfully reinvent themselves and emerge stronger by embracing AI, it is still too early to identify the eventual winners. On the other hand, several large IT companies could continue to underperform and may even lose their place in benchmark indices like Nifty 50 over time. Investors should therefore avoid trying to catch a falling knife, because cheap valuations alone do not always make a good investment.

Neo Asset today announced the final close of the Neo Real Asset Yield Fund, an open-ended Category III Alternative Investment Fund, at its target corpus of ₹1,000 crore, completing the raise in under five months. The fund invests across privately and publicly listed REITs and InvITs, and has already deployed over 80% of its committed capital.

Neo was the first platform to raise and deploy capital through this structure. The category has since seen additional managers launch similar strategies, several of which remain in active fundraising or early deployment.

Commenting on the close, Shajikumar Devakar, Co-Founder – Neo Group, said:

“This fund was created to give our clients a dedicated way to allocate to real assets -InvITs and REITs – within their broader portfolios. The strong response reflects growing demand to diversify beyond traditional fixed income and equity allocations. This strategy was designed to provide institutional-grade access to high-quality privately listed InvITs, typically reserved for large institutions, alongside the best public REIT and InvIT opportunities – combining premium access with liquidity.”

The Centre has cleared IndianOil LNG's ₹3,400 crore Ennore terminal expansion, doubling capacity to 10 MTPA and boosting gas supply across South India.

Mynd Fintech, the supply chain finance subsidiary of M1xchange, a leading RBI licensed TReDS platform. Mynd Fintech has entered into a strategic partnership with C2FO India Technologies Pvt. Ltd., marking one of the most significant partnerships in India’s early payment and supply chain finance space. With this partnership, the combined early payment business is expected to cross ₹50,000 crore in annual transaction volumes this year, serving over 140 large corporates , including nearly half of the Nifty 50 and more than 200,000+ suppliers. The deal makes Mynd Fintech the leading player in India’s Early Payment ecosystem.

JSW Steel: Fitch Ratings upgrades company’s long-term issuer default rating and senior unsecured bonds to BB+ Positive outlook.

SIS Ltd traded flat at Rs 426.45 on the NSE; Updater Services were down 2% to Rs 190.95. SIS had acquired an additional 2 lakh shares of Updater Services.
